prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, landscape orientation
Primary request: Deadlands / Weird West inspired world map for the terrain type dual_continent.
Scene/backdrop: two major frontier continents separated by uneven haunted seas and narrow straits, weird west territories, ghost-rock deposits, railroad corridors, boom towns, desert badlands, haunted mesas, prairie grasslands, pine highlands, flooded maze coastlines and supernatural dead zones.
Style/medium: high-resolution illustrated atlas and old frontier cartography, sepia parchment, ink wash and watercolor relief, engraved linework, dusty western horror atmosphere, ghost-rock steam-tech silhouettes.
Composition/framing: wide landscape world map, asymmetric continents of different sizes; the ocean separation is diagonal and S-shaped with C-shaped gulfs and jagged straits, not a vertical divider; include map border, compass, mine/railroad iconography and subtle territorial markings with no readable text.
Geography constraints: coastlines must be realistic and complex with peninsulas, bays, gulfs, capes, broken coasts, curving shores, deltas, tidal marshes and inland seas; landforms vary from deserts and canyons to alpine ranges, forests, swamps, plains and cursed wastelands.
Avoid: simple two-blob layout, mirrored continents, clean vertical split, modern road map, readable labels, UI, watermark, logo, real-world map copy.
